title: Implement MeshCore-compatible local transport surface
description: Why this exists: MeshCore clients need to discover/connect over their expected local transport before app-level flows can work. What needs to be done: implement or adapt the Zephyr-side BLE, serial, or IPC surface identified by research, including service/characteristic IDs or serial framing, advertising/name behavior, session setup, MTU/packet limits, reconnect behavior, and coexistence with LICHEN Native and Meshtastic app adapters. Acceptance criteria: transport can be enabled by Kconfig without board-specific code; RX/TX paths feed the MeshCore adapter; reconnect and MTU/fragmentation behavior are tested or explicitly scoped; unrelated transports remain unaffected.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 BLE coexistence: implement MeshCore BLE as a separate mutually exclusive product mode at first. Use exact NUS UUIDs, raw MeshCore inner frames on BLE, no serial/TCP length prefix, MeshCore-compatible advertising name only in this mode, BT_MAX_CONN=1/session epoch boundaries, and no direct RF/link calls from BLE transport.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: transport implementation must carry BLE security/PIN requirements, not just NUS/raw framing. Require encrypted MITM pairing where supported; do not reuse native trust pins, OSCORE keys, link keys, or provisioning secrets; generate/configure/persist a MeshCore compatibility PIN per policy; guard or suppress DEVICE_INFO PIN exposure until client expectation and security review accept it; implement CMD_SET_DEVICE_PIN only with persistent storage and zero/valid 6-digit validation, returning ERR_UNSUPPORTED or ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG as specified.
Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: add enforceable Zephyr BLE security configuration for MeshCore mode. Production MeshCore BLE must not use plain NUS GATT permissions; RX/TX access must require encrypted authenticated pairing/passkey where supported, with insecure plain permissions limited to native_sim/test builds and labeled as such. Default advertised name should use MeshCore-LICHEN or another prefix proven by project-LICHEN-9c4y.11, not LICHEN-MC by default.
